---
title: "Research"
---

## Research Program

My research examines how learners, educators, and institutions develop the capacity to understand, teach, and make decisions about complex socio-environmental systems. I integrate discipline-based education research, mixed methods research, and evaluation approaches to study learning, instructional practice, and educational change across STEM and sustainability education contexts.

## Research Strands
### Water Literacy, Systems Thinking, and Decision-Making
I study how learners reason about water resources and other socio-environmental systems involving uncertainty, tradeoffs, and multiple stakeholders. This work examines water literacy, systems thinking, sustainability education, stakeholder reasoning, and structured decision-making, including the use of models, simulations, multi-criteria decision-making, and game theory in environmental and water education.

### Pedagogical Content Knowledge, Teacher Learning, and Instructional Design
I investigate how undergraduate students who will become elementary teachers develop the knowledge, practices, and instructional approaches needed to support meaningful learning in science and environmental education. This strand includes research on pedagogical content knowledge (PCK), professional development, curriculum design, translanguaging approaches, hydrologic, climate and Earth systems education, and adoption of instructional resources.

### Project Evaluation, Educational Systems, and Institutional Learning
I conduct mixed-methods evaluation research examining educational programs, collaborative initiatives, and institutional systems. This work includes studies of program implementation, organizational learning, professional development, workforce development partnerships, collaboration networks, and educational outcomes using surveys, interviews, learning analytics, and social network analysis.

## Peer-reviewed journal articles

Liu, J., Hoover, K. S., **Mostacedo-Marasovic, S. J.**, Suravarjhula, S., Linga, S., Forbes, C. T., & Moise, T. (2026). Investigating the longitudinal effectiveness of a regional semiconductor consortium: a mixed-methods study of members’ perspectives and student enrollment trends. *Journal of Workplace Learning, 38*(2), 145-161. <https://doi.org/10.1108/JWL-07-2025-0248>

**Mostacedo-Marasovic, S. J.**, Raub, K. R., McCay, D. H., & Forbes, C. T. (2025). Instructors’ needs for teaching and mentoring undergraduate students in the water sciences. *Journal of Contemporary Water Research and Education, 182*(2). <https://opensiuc.lib.siu.edu/jcwre/vol182/iss2/2/>

**Mostacedo-Marasovic, S. J.**, Costello, B., Olsen, A. A., & Forbes, C. T. (2025). Enhancing teachers’ capacities for technology-rich climate education: Studying the impact of a virtual professional development program for secondary science teachers. *Contemporary Issues in Technology and Teacher Education, 25*(3). <https://citejournal.org/volume-25/issue-3-25/science/enhancing-teachers-capacities-for-technology-rich-climate-education-studying-the-impact-of-a-virtual-professional-development-program-for-secondary-science-teachers>

**Mostacedo-Marasovic, S. J.**, Ahmad, J., Chakravarthy, S., McCullough, T., Park, J. Y., Forbes, C. T., & Eisma, J. (2025). Enhancing undergraduate research readiness in water and energy: A one-week bootcamp integrating FAIR data principles, Python, and machine learning. *Journal of College Science Teaching*, 1–8. <https://doi.org/10.1080/0047231X.2025.2508802>

**Mostacedo-Marasovic, S. J.**, Costello, B., Olsen, A. A., & Forbes, C. T. (2025). Supporting secondary teachers’ interest in adopting climate-related curricular resources. *Journal of Geoscience Education*, 1–13. <https://doi.org/10.1080/10899995.2025.2506029>

**Mostacedo-Marasovic, S. J.**, White, H. C., & Forbes, C. T. (2025). The Food–Energy–Water Nexus: Using Hydroviz to support undergraduate students’ systems thinking about complex socio-hydrologic issues. *Interdisciplinary Journal of Environmental and Science Education, 21*(2), e2506. <https://doi.org/10.29333/ijese/15901>

**Mostacedo-Marasovic, S. J.**, White, H. C., & Forbes, C. T. (2024). The Food–Energy–Water Nexus: Assessing undergraduate students’ decision making about complex socio-hydrologic issues supported by Hydroviz. *Journal of Geoscience Education, 73*(3), 318–331. <https://doi.org/10.1080/10899995.2024.2358726>

**Mostacedo-Marasovic, S. J.**, & Forbes, C. T. (2024). Faculty development program about the Food–Energy–Water Nexus: Supporting faculty’s adoption of a curricular module and program evaluation. *International Journal of Sustainability in Higher Education, 25*(8), 1837–1853. <https://doi.org/10.1108/IJSHE-06-2023-0262>

**Mostacedo-Marasovic, S. J.**, Olsen, A. A., & Forbes, C. T. (2024). Supporting secondary students’ understanding of Earth’s climate system and global climate change using EzGCM: A cross-sectional study. *Journal of Science Education and Technology, 33*, 178–194. <https://doi.org/10.1007/s10956-023-10067-8>

**Mostacedo-Marasovic, S. J.**, Mott, B. C., White, H. C., & Forbes, C. T. (2022). Towards water literacy: Analysis of standards for teaching and learning about water on Earth. *Journal of Geoscience Education, 71*(2), 192–207. <https://doi.org/10.1080/10899995.2022.2112490>

**Mostacedo-Marasovic, S. J.**, Mott, B. C., White, H. C., & Forbes, C. T. (2022). Towards water literacy: An interdisciplinary analysis of standards for teaching and learning about humans and water. *Disciplinary and Interdisciplinary Science Education Research, 4*(25). <https://doi.org/10.1186/s43031-022-00065-y>

**Mostacedo-Marasovic, S. J.**, Lally, D., Petitt, D. N., White, H., & Forbes, C. T. (2022). Supporting undergraduate students’ developing water literacy during a global pandemic: A longitudinal study. *Disciplinary and Interdisciplinary Science Education Research, 4*(7). <https://doi.org/10.1186/s43031-022-00049-y>

## Book chapter

Olsen, A. A., **Mostacedo-Marasovic, S. J.**, & Forbes, C. T. (2023). Applying Rasch modeling to a global climate change concept knowledge assessment for secondary students. In X. Liu & W. J. Boone (Eds.), *Advances in applications of Rasch measurement in science education*. Springer. <https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-031-28776-3_8>

## Grants & Projects

- **NSF IUSE (Co-PI)** — Enhancing Undergraduate Education About Stakeholder Competition and Cooperation in Water Systems Using Game Theory (PI: C. T. Forbes). Award No. 2517117.
